## [00:00 - 01:50]

Hey, welcome to the characteristics, the traits of great salespeople. I wanted to put this together for those of you who no longer choose to accept average or even good or even be on top of your company. This segment, these many segments to follow are about greatness. What great traits, the traits of those, those characteristics of those people that have attained what other people consider greatness, that exceptional state, that extraordinary condition that goes or gets labeled to those who excel at a completely different level, that are in a different playing field, that are looked at by their peers as like, oh my god, that guy made it and he made it big. So that's what I want to talk about here, those characteristics, those traits, those things that the great salespeople that I've met over the last 30 years, I've been privy to meet tens of thousands, hundreds of thousands of salespeople, sales organizations, vice presidents of sales, sales managers, guys that were just unbelievable at negotiating, and some guys that were just, I mean, crazy great at closing transactions, and then other guys that were just like unbelievable at follow up and prospecting and customer development, and then other guys that were just great at building value and selling their product. And then I've met others that were great at all of them. And that's what I want to share with you here, some inspiration about what it takes to be great, exceptional, extraordinary, not average, not mediocre. So this is of interest to you. I encourage you to watch this once a month. There's a lot of segments in here with a lot of characteristics. I think you'll find it motivating, inspiring, and something to shoot for. Hey, be great and enjoy the characteristics of great salespeople.
